content.problemStatement:
Danya has a rooted tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN). Node 111 is the root and for each iii (2‚â§i‚â§N2 \le i \le N2‚â§i‚â§N), the parent of the iii-th node is pip_ipi‚Äã. For each valid iii, the iii-th node also has a value aia_iai‚Äã.

Danya really loves his tree and wants to play with it. You should answer his QQQ queries. In each query:


You are given two integers XXX and DDD.
Consider the set SSS of all nodes vvv that lie in the subtree of node XXX (including XXX) such that the distance between XXX and vvv does not exceed DDD.
The answer to this query is the number of different values ava_vav‚Äã among all v‚ààSv \in Sv‚ààS.
constraints: 1‚â§pi‚â§i‚àí11 \le p_i \le i-11‚â§pi‚Äã‚â§i‚àí1 for each valid iii
1‚â§ai‚â§N1 \le a_i \le N1‚â§ai‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
**Subtask #2 (24 points):** the tree is a bamboo, i.e. pi=i‚àí1p_i = i-1pi‚Äã=i‚àí1 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 space-separated integers p2,p3,‚Ä¶,pNp_2, p_3, \ldots, p_Np2‚Äã,p3‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äã.
The fourth line contains a single integer QQQ.
QQQ lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and ddd describing a query; the parameters XXX and DDD can be computed in the following way:

Let's denote the answer to the previous query by ansansans (ans=0ans = 0ans=0 if this is the first query).
Then, X=x‚äïansX = x \oplus ansX=x‚äïans and D=d‚äïansD = d \oplus ansD=d‚äïans, where ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operator.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the answer to the query.
